questionHtml:
How much ethanol contains 1.20 × 1024 atoms of carbon?

Avogadro’s constant, L or NA: 6.02 × 1023 mol−1


A.  0.333 mol

B.  0.500 mol

C.  1.00 mol

D.  2.00 mol

meta.title: How much ethanol contains 1.20 × 1024 atoms of carbon? Avogadro’s constant, L or NA: 6.02 × 1023 mol−1 A. 0.333 mol B. 0.500 mol C. 1.00 mol D. 2.00 mol
breadcrumbLabels: Structure 1. Models of the particulate nature of matter
Structure 1.4—Counting particles by mass: The mole

Structure 1.4.1—The mole (mol) is the SI unit of amount of substance. One mole contains exactly the number of elementary entities given by the Avogadro constant. Convert the amount of substance, n, to the number of specified elementary entities.
